title: Data-accuracy reconciliation + provenance-completeness suite (staleness flags, unit/identity checks)
context: A wrong water-safety figure drives real-world action. The data-accuracy reconciliation suite and provenance-completeness gate are the CI enforcement points for two project-critical invariants: every published figure must reconcile to its cited snapshot, and every published figure must carry a complete citation. Stale data must be surfaced honestly with staleness flags, never silently corrected or hidden. Unit and contaminant-identity mismatches (e.g., ppb vs. mg/L confusion, mislabeled contaminants) are a common class of water-data error and must be caught before publication.
objective: Implement the CI data-accuracy reconciliation suite and provenance-completeness gate (pipeline/ or ci/): (1) fail CI if any published figure does not reconcile to its cited snapshot (value, unit, contaminant identity, sample period); (2) fail CI if any published figure lacks a complete citation (publisher, dataset, URL, retrievedDate, sourceVintage, license, checksum); (3) unit and contaminant-identity sanity checks across the normalized dataset; (4) staleness flags on results/violations based on sample period + snapshot date, surfaced honestly in explainer output.
acceptanceCriteria: CI fails if any published figure does not reconcile to the value in its cited snapshot (matching value, unit, contaminant identity, and sample period)
CI fails if any published figure lacks any required Citation field (publisher, dataset, url, retrievedDate, sourceVintage, sourceLicense, checksum)
Unit sanity checks catch mismatches (e.g., ppb vs. mg/L confusion) before merge
Contaminant-identity checks catch mislabeled or unmapped contaminants before merge
Staleness flags are generated based on sample period + snapshot date and are surfaced in explainer output, not hidden
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: PR adding CI reconciliation and provenance-completeness gate scripts/tests; CI workflow configuration ensuring all published figures are reconciled and fully cited.
